answer:
\boxed{J}

The **PSW (Program Status Word)** is a register in many processors (including IBM mainframes) that stores critical system status information, such as the **location counter (program counter)**, **interrupt status**, and **protection keys**. The three following lines typically display the contents of the **registers at the time of an interruption or error**, helping diagnose execution issues. This matches the context of a program printout showing processor state details.
